16: // sessionConnectionCleanupJob defines a periodic job that monitors workers for
17: // loss of connection and terminates connections on workers that have
18: // not sent a heartbeat in a significant period of time.
19: //
20: // Relevant connections are simply marked as disconnected in the
21: // database. Connections will be independently terminated by the
22: // worker, or the event of a synchronization issue between the two,
23: // the controller will win out and order that the connections be
24: // closed on the worker.
25: type sessionConnectionCleanupJob struct {
26: 	connectionRepoFn common.ConnectionRepoFactory
27: 
28: 	// The amount of time to give disconnected workers before marking
29: 	// their connections as closed.
30: 	gracePeriod time.Duration
31: 
32: 	// The total number of connections closed in the last run.
33: 	totalClosed int
34: }
35: 
36: // newSessionConnectionCleanupJob instantiates the session cleanup job.
37: func newSessionConnectionCleanupJob(
38: 	connectionRepoFn common.ConnectionRepoFactory,
39: 	gracePeriod time.Duration,
40: ) (*sessionConnectionCleanupJob, error) {
41: 	const op = "controller.newNewSessionConnectionCleanupJob"
42: 	switch {
43: 	case connectionRepoFn == nil:
44: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing connectionRepoFn")
45: 	case gracePeriod < session.DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace:
46: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(
47: 			errors.InvalidParameter, op, fmt.Sprintf("invalid gracePeriod, must be greater than %s", session.DeadWorkerConnCloseMinGrace))
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	return &sessionConnectionCleanupJob{
51: 		connectionRepoFn: connectionRepoFn,
52: 		gracePeriod:      gracePeriod,
53: 	}, nil
54: }
55: 
56: // Name returns a short, unique name for the job.
57: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Name() string { return "session_cleanup" }
58: 
59: // Description returns the description for the job.
60: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Description() string {
61: 	return "Clean up session connections from disconnected workers"
62: }
63: 
64: // NextRunIn returns the next run time after a job is completed.
65: //
66: // The next run time is defined for sessionConnectionCleanupJob as one second.
67: // This is because the job should run continuously to terminate
68: // connections as soon as a worker has not reported in for a long
69: // enough time. Only one job will ever run at once, so there is no
70: // reason why it cannot run again immediately.
71: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) NextRunIn(_ context.Context) (time.Duration, error) {
72: 	return time.Second, nil
73: }
74: 
75: // Status returns the status of the running job.
76: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Status() scheduler.JobStatus {
77: 	return scheduler.JobStatus{
78: 		Completed: j.totalClosed,
79: 		Total:     j.totalClosed,
80: 	}
81: }
82: 
83: // Run executes the job.
84: func (j *sessionConnectionCleanupJob) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
85: 	const op = "controller.(sessionConnectionCleanupJob).Run"
86: 	j.totalClosed = 0
87: 
88: 	// Load repos.
89: 	connectionRepo, err := j.connectionRepoFn()
90: 	if err != nil {
91: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error getting session repo"))
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	// Run the atomic dead worker cleanup job.
95: 	results, err := connectionRepo.CloseConnectionsForDeadWorkers(ctx, j.gracePeriod)
96: 	if err != nil {
97: 		return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	for _, result := range results {
101: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("worker has not reported status within acceptable grace period, all connections closed"),
102: 			event.WithInfo(
103: 				"private_id", result.ServerId,
104: 				"update_time", result.LastUpdateTime,
105: 				"grace_period_seconds", j.gracePeriod.Seconds(),
106: 				"number_connections_closed", result.NumberConnectionsClosed,
107: 			))
108: 
109: 		j.totalClosed += result.NumberConnectionsClosed
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	return nil
113: }

49: 	worker2 := session.TestWorker(t, conn, wrapper, session.WithServerId("worker2"))
50: 
51: 	// Create a few sessions on each, activate, and authorize a connection
52: 	var connIds []string
53: 	connIdsByWorker := make(map[string][]string)
54: 	for i := 0; i < numConns; i++ {
55: 		serverId := worker1.PrivateId

59: 		sess := session.TestDefaultSession(t, conn, wrapper, iamRepo, session.WithServerId(serverId), session.WithDbOpts(db.WithSkipVetForWrite(true)))

71: 		}
72: 	}

189: 				external_host_plugins.WithLogger(pluginLogger.Named(pluginType)),
190: 			)
191: 			if err != nil {
192: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("error creating %s host plugin: %w", pluginType, err)
193: 			}
194: 			conf.ShutdownFuncs = append(conf.ShutdownFuncs, cleanup)
195: 			if _, err := conf.RegisterHostPlugin(ctx, pluginType, client, hostplugin.WithDescription(fmt.Sprintf("Built-in %s host plugin", enabledPlugin.String()))); err != nil {
196: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("error registering %s host plugin: %w", pluginType, err)
197: 			}
198: 		}
199: 	}
200: 
201: 	if conf.HostPlugins == nil {
202: 		conf.HostPlugins = make(map[string]plugin.HostPluginServiceClient)
203: 	}
204: 
205: 	// Set up repo stuff
206: 	dbase := db.New(c.conf.Database)
207: 	kmsRepo, err := kms.NewRepository(dbase, dbase)
208: 	if err != nil {
209: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error creating kms repository: %w", err)
210: 	}
211: 	c.kms, err = kms.NewKms(kmsRepo)
212: 	if err != nil {
213: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error creating kms cache: %w", err)
214: 	}
215: 	if err := c.kms.AddExternalWrappers(
216: 		ctx,
217: 		kms.WithRootWrapper(c.conf.RootKms),
218: 		kms.WithWorkerAuthWrapper(c.conf.WorkerAuthKms),
219: 		kms.WithRecoveryWrapper(c.conf.RecoveryKms),
220: 	); err != nil {
221: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error adding config keys to kms: %w", err)
222: 	}
223: 
224: 	// we need to get all the scopes so we can reconcile the DEKs for each scope.
225: 	iamRepo, err := iam.NewRepository(dbase, dbase, c.kms, iam.WithRandomReader(c.conf.SecureRandomReader))
226: 	if err != nil {
227: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to initialize iam repository: %w", err)
228: 	}
229: 	allScopes, err := iamRepo.ListScopesRecursively(ctx, scope.Global.String())
230: 	if err != nil {
231: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error listing all scopes for reconciling keys: %w", err)
232: 	}
233: 	reconcileScopeIds := make([]string, 0, len(allScopes))
234: 	for _, s := range allScopes {
235: 		reconcileScopeIds = append(reconcileScopeIds, s.PublicId)
236: 	}
237: 	if err := c.kms.ReconcileKeys(ctx, c.conf.SecureRandomReader, kms.WithScopeIds(reconcileScopeIds...)); err != nil {
238: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reconciling kms keys: %w", err)
239: 	}
240: 
241: 	// now that the kms is configured, we can get the audit wrapper and rotate
242: 	// the eventer audit wrapper, so the emitted events can include encrypt and
243: 	// hmac-sha256 data
244: 	auditWrapper, err := c.kms.GetWrapper(ctx, scope.Global.String(), kms.KeyPurposeAudit)
245: 	if err != nil {
246: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error getting audit wrapper from kms: %w", err)
247: 	}
248: 	if err := c.conf.Eventer.RotateAuditWrapper(ctx, auditWrapper); err != nil {
249: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error rotating eventer audit wrapper: %w", err)
250: 	}
251: 	jobRepoFn := func() (*job.Repository, error) {
252: 		return job.NewRepository(dbase, dbase, c.kms)
253: 	}
254: 	// TODO: the RunJobsLimit is temporary until a better fix gets in. This
255: 	// currently caps the scheduler at running 10 jobs per interval.
256: 	schedulerOpts := []scheduler.Option{scheduler.WithRunJobsLimit(10)}
257: 	if c.conf.RawConfig.Controller.SchedulerRunJobInterval > 0 {
258: 		schedulerOpts = append(schedulerOpts, scheduler.WithRunJobsInterval(c.conf.RawConfig.Controller.SchedulerRunJobInterval))
259: 	}
260: 	c.scheduler, err = scheduler.New(c.conf.RawConfig.Controller.Name, jobRepoFn, schedulerOpts...)
261: 	if err != nil {
262: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error creating new scheduler: %w", err)
263: 	}
264: 	c.IamRepoFn = func() (*iam.Repository, error) {
265: 		return iam.NewRepository(dbase, dbase, c.kms, iam.WithRandomReader(c.conf.SecureRandomReader))
266: 	}
267: 	c.StaticHostRepoFn = func() (*static.Repository, error) {
268: 		return static.NewRepository(dbase, dbase, c.kms)
269: 	}
270: 	c.PluginHostRepoFn = func() (*pluginhost.Repository, error) {

272: 	}
273: 	c.HostPluginRepoFn = func() (*host.Repository, error) {

275: 	}

278: 			authtoken.WithTokenTimeToLiveDuration(c.conf.RawConfig.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToLiveDuration),
279: 			authtoken.WithTokenTimeToStaleDuration(c.conf.RawConfig.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToStaleDuration))
280: 	}
281: 	c.VaultCredentialRepoFn = func() (*vault.Repository, error) {
282: 		return vault.NewRepository(dbase, dbase, c.kms, c.scheduler)
283: 	}
284: 	c.ServersRepoFn = func() (*servers.Repository, error) {

286: 	}

292: 	}

294: 		return target.NewRepository(dbase, dbase, c.kms)
295: 	}
296: 	c.SessionRepoFn = func() (*session.Repository, error) {
297: 		return session.NewRepository(dbase, dbase, c.kms)
298: 	}
299: 	c.ConnectionRepoFn = func() (*session.ConnectionRepository, error) {
300: 		return session.NewConnectionRepository(ctx, dbase, dbase, c.kms)
301: 	}
302: 	return c, nil
303: }
304: 
305: func (c *Controller) Start() error {
306: 	const op = "controller.(Controller).Start"
307: 	if c.started.Load() {
308: 		event.WriteSysEvent(context.TODO(), op, "already started, skipping")
309: 		return nil
310: 	}
311: 	c.baseContext, c.baseCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
312: 	if err := c.registerJobs(); err != nil {
313: 		return fmt.Errorf("error registering jobs: %w", err)
314: 	}
315: 	if err := c.startListeners(); err != nil {
316: 		return fmt.Errorf("error starting controller listeners: %w", err)
317: 	}
318: 
319: 	// Upsert server before starting tickers and scheduler to ensure the server exists
320: 	if err := c.upsertServer(c.baseContext); err != nil {
321: 		return fmt.Errorf("error upserting server: %w", err)
322: 	}
323: 	if err := c.scheduler.Start(c.baseContext, c.schedulerWg); err != nil {
324: 		return fmt.Errorf("error starting scheduler: %w", err)
325: 	}
326: 
327: 	c.tickerWg.Add(5)
328: 	go func() {
329: 		defer c.tickerWg.Done()
330: 		c.startStatusTicking(c.baseContext)
331: 	}()
332: 	go func() {
333: 		defer c.tickerWg.Done()
334: 		c.startNonceCleanupTicking(c.baseContext)
335: 	}()
336: 	go func() {
337: 		defer c.tickerWg.Done()
338: 		c.startTerminateCompletedSessionsTicking(c.baseContext)
339: 	}()
340: 	go func() {
341: 		defer c.tickerWg.Done()
342: 		c.startCloseExpiredPendingTokens(c.baseContext)
343: 	}()
344: 	go func() {
345: 		defer c.tickerWg.Done()
346: 		c.started.Store(true)
347: 	}()
348: 
349: 	return nil
350: }
351: 
352: func (c *Controller) registerJobs() error {
353: 	rw := db.New(c.conf.Database)
354: 	if err := vault.RegisterJobs(c.baseContext, c.scheduler, rw, rw, c.kms); err != nil {
355: 		return err
356: 	}
357: 	if err := pluginhost.RegisterJobs(c.baseContext, c.scheduler, rw, rw, c.kms, c.conf.HostPlugins); err != nil {
358: 		return err
359: 	}
360: 
361: 	if err := c.registerSessionConnectionCleanupJob(); err != nil {
362: 		return err
363: 	}
364: 
365: 	return nil
366: }
367: 
368: // registerSessionConnectionCleanupJob is a helper method to abstract
369: // registering the session connection cleanup job specifically.
370: func (c *Controller) registerSessionConnectionCleanupJob() error {
371: 	sessionConnectionCleanupJob, err := newSessionConnectionCleanupJob(c.ConnectionRepoFn, c.conf.StatusGracePeriodDuration)
372: 	if err != nil {
373: 		return fmt.Errorf("error creating session cleanup job: %w", err)
374: 	}
375: 	if err = c.scheduler.RegisterJob(c.baseContext, sessionConnectionCleanupJob); err != nil {
376: 		return fmt.Errorf("error registering session cleanup job: %w", err)
377: 	}
378: 
379: 	return nil
380: }
381: 
382: func (c *Controller) Shutdown() error {
383: 	const op = "controller.(Controller).Shutdown"
384: 	if !c.started.Load() {
385: 		event.WriteSysEvent(context.TODO(), op, "already shut down, skipping")
386: 	}
387: 	defer c.started.Store(false)
388: 	c.baseCancel()
389: 	if err := c.stopServersAndListeners(); err != nil {
390: 		return fmt.Errorf("error stopping controller servers and listeners: %w", err)
391: 	}
392: 	c.schedulerWg.Wait()
393: 	c.tickerWg.Wait()
394: 	if c.conf.Eventer != nil {
395: 		if err := c.conf.Eventer.FlushNodes(context.Background()); err != nil {
396: 			return fmt.Errorf("error flushing controller eventer nodes: %w", err)
397: 		}
398: 	}
399: 	return nil
400: }
